behavior_summary: U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit is a small process invoked when an actor is struck; it immediately delegates handling to a WATER subsystem routine and then yields/suspends.
The routine appears to trigger a water-related response to the hit (e.g., clear water state, produce a splash or visual/physical reaction) but performs no local state changes or global flag updates.
The process accepts two parameters (uword, word) which are present but not manipulated locally in this snippet; they are likely passed to or used by the WATER routine.
notable_control_flow: Entry: process runs on a 'got hit' event for the actor.
Single-step flow: U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit -> call WATER::func0080 -> suspend/yield.
No branching, no reads/writes of global flags, and no internal computation observed in the snippet.
open_questions: What is the exact behavior of WATER::func0080 (e.g., clear water, spawn splash animation, modify physics)?
What do the two parameters (param1: uword, param2: word) represent (attacker id, damage amount, hit flags, or coordinates)?
Is U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit invoked for all actors of this type or only for specific actor classes (player vs NPC)?
